data.title: Analysis: Bank of America warns of risks in the crypto market; traders closely watch key inflation data this week.
data.description: Bitcoin and gold have diverged in recent months, with Bitcoin prices falling while gold prices have surged. Traders are on high alert following warnings from the CEO of Bank of America about risks in the cryptocurrency market, anticipating higher-than-expected inflation data this week, which could trigger warnings of "unprecedented stagflation." Economists at Barclays and Morgan Stanley have reportedly revised their forecasts for the December U.S. Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) price index, to be released on Thursday, to 2.8% and 2.9% respectively. This data could reignite concerns about so-called "stagflation"—weak economic growth coupled with soaring prices. (Forbes)

data.title: Paradex has resumed normal operation.
data.description: According to the Paradex website's status page update, its blockchain status was restored at 20:13. Previously, Paradex experienced a system outage earlier today due to database maintenance/migration issues (including an abnormal drop in BTC price to zero and triggering numerous forced liquidations). The official announcement subsequently stated that the blockchain status would be rolled back to block 1604710 (the normal state before approximately 04:27 UTC), and all user accounts would be uniformly restored to the state before maintenance.

data.title: A hedge fund tycoon warns: If the Federal Reserve becomes a "puppet," the United States will face epic punishment.
data.description: David Picton, head of Picton Investments, said the bond market would quickly punish the US if President Trump appoints a Federal Reserve chairman perceived as overly subservient, while precious metals remain a good hedge against political volatility. He noted that "there's some correlation between the number of Trump's posts and the performance of currency devaluation trades (i.e., gold, silver, and these commodity-based hedges)." Picton's firm manages approximately C$16.6 billion (US$11.9 billion) in assets. He said he doesn't believe the Fed will ultimately lose its independence, but Trump's repeated verbal attacks on Powell are "extremely unhelpful." (Jinshi)

data.title: Immunefi CEO: On-chain security is improving, but "humans" are becoming the biggest vulnerability in crypto security.
data.description: In a recent interview, Immunefi CEO Mitchell Amador stated that while cryptocurrency losses continue to rise, on-chain security is improving. 2025 was the worst year for hacking on record, but the biggest security failures did not stem from on-chain code, but rather from operational errors in Web2, such as passwords, private keys, infected devices, and human error. As code becomes increasingly difficult to exploit, the primary attack surface for crypto security in 2026 will be humans, making the human factor a vulnerability that Web3 participants must prioritize. This view aligns with the findings of a Chainalysis report, which shows that cryptocurrency losses due to fraud and scams reached approximately $17 billion in 2025. Identity theft scams increased by 1400% year-over-year, while AI-driven scams were 450% more profitable than traditional methods. (CoinDesk)

data.title: Analysts: Coinbase Bitcoin premium/discount metric falls to near one-year low, dragged down by holiday selling by US whales.
data.description: Analyst Mignolet stated that the Coinbase Premium (CPG) metric hit a near 12-month low, stemming from selling by US whales during the US holiday season, rather than from Bitcoin spot ETFs. Data shows that the current 30-day average CPG for Bitcoin has fallen to approximately -63.85, the lowest level since January 2025, indicating stronger selling pressure from US investors than in global markets. The CPG primarily tracks the price difference between the Bitcoin/USD trading pair on Coinbase and the BTC/USDT trading pair on Binance. Technically, the Bitcoin daily chart has formed a rising wedge pattern, which increases the downside risk and could potentially lead to a drop to around $80,000. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: EU to Convene Emergency Summit on U.S. Tariff Threats
data.description:
According to PANews, EU Commission spokesperson Olof Gill announced on January 19 that the European Union will hold an emergency summit on January 22 in Brussels, Belgium. The meeting will address U.S. President Donald Trump's announcement of increased tariffs on European countries opposing the U.S. acquisition of Greenland. The summit aims to evaluate potential countermeasures by the EU.

Gill stated that communication between the EU and the U.S. continues at various levels in response to Trump's tariff threats. Intensive consultations are also underway within the EU to discuss possible responses with member states. The EU is committed to making every necessary effort to protect its interests.

Gill emphasized that the EU's primary goal is engagement rather than escalation. However, he noted that if the U.S. implements the proposed tariffs, the EU has tools at its disposal and is prepared to respond.

data.title: If you invested $1,000 in Robert Kiyosaki’s gold, silver, and Bitcoin portfolio in 2026, here’s your return so far
data.description:
Despite years of gloomy forecasts, best-selling personal finance author and prominent ‘finfluencer’ Robert Kiyosaki’s favorite assets, gold, silver, and Bitcoin (BTC), are all up in early 2026, meaning a $1,000 investment at the start of the year would already be showing a double-digit gain. To see how his strategy is holding up, we calculated the return on a simple $1,000 investment made on January 1, 2026. The Robert Kiyosaki portfolio 2026 performance In the first 19 days of the year, Bitcoin rose 6.59% from $87,412 on New Year’s Day to $93,169 at press time
source: https://finbold.com/if-you-invested-1000-in-robert-kiyosakis-gold-silver-and-bitcoin-portfolio-in-2026-heres-your-return-so-far/

data.title: NYSE Develops Platform for Tokenized Securities Trading and Settlement
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, on January 19, the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE), part of the Intercontinental Exchange Group, announced the development of a platform for tokenized securities trading and on-chain settlement, seeking regulatory approval for its implementation.

The NYSE's new digital platform aims to offer a tokenized trading experience with features such as 24/7 operations, instant settlement, order placement in USD amounts, and stablecoin-based fund transfers. The design integrates the NYSE Pillar matching engine with a blockchain-based post-trade system, enabling multi-chain settlement and custody capabilities.

Upon receiving regulatory approval, the platform will facilitate a new NYSE trading venue, supporting tokenized stock trading interchangeable with traditional issued securities, as well as native digital securities tokens. Tokenized shareholders will retain dividend and governance rights akin to traditional shareholders. The venue is designed to adhere to established market structure principles, providing non-discriminatory access to all qualified broker-dealers for distribution.

The launch of the NYSE tokenized securities platform is part of the Intercontinental Exchange's broader digital strategy, which includes preparing its clearing infrastructure for round-the-clock trading and exploring the integration of tokenized collateral. The Intercontinental Exchange is collaborating with banks such as BNY Mellon and Citigroup to support tokenized deposits within its clearinghouse, aiding clearing members in transferring and managing funds outside traditional banking hours, fulfilling margin obligations, and accommodating financial needs across different jurisdictions and time zones.
